title: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Mounted Units
h1: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Mounted Units
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Mounted Units Cavallo Hoof Boots for Mounted Units provide the traction, comfort, and protection horses need to perform safely on patrol. From busy streets to rugged trails, Cavallo boots reduce concussion, improve grip, and support long-term hoof health. Mounted police and patrol teams rely on Cavallos to keep their horses sound, focused, and ready for action. With less fatigue, fewer injuries, and lower vet bills, more units are making the switch. Cavallo also offers special discounts to mounted units, making protection more affordable than ever. Discover how Cavallo Hoof Boots for mounted units deliver real results when duty calls. title: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Arthritis
text: Cavallo Hoof Boots for Arthritis Cavallo Hoof Boots for arthritis offer aging and arthritic horses the comfort and support they need to stay active and pain-free. Cavallo's innovative boots reduce concussion, improve traction, and distribute pressure more evenly. These are key benefits for managing joint stiffness and soreness. Whether your horse is still being ridden or enjoying retirement, Cavallo Hoof Boots help ease movement and promote circulation, aiding long-term joint health. Backed by scientific research and trusted by horse owners worldwide, they’re easy to use on both barefoot and shod horses. Keep your horse moving comfortably with Cavallo - because every step should feel good! With advanced sole technology, Cavallo boots cushion every step, allowing horses to move more freely, even when stiffness or joint pain might otherwise hold them back. title: Transitioning to Barefoot with Cavallo
h1: Transitioning Your Horse to Barefoot with Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: Transitioning Your Horse to Barefoot with Cavallo Hoof Boots Transitioning to barefoot with Cavallo Hoof Boots is a powerful step toward restoring your horse’s natural movement, circulation, and hoof strength. Whether your horse is coming out of metal shoes or recovering from lameness, Cavallo boots provide the comfort and protection needed to make the transition smooth and successful. With shock-absorbing soles, easy-to-use closures, and proven results, Cavallo supports every step of the barefoot transition. Make the switch with confidence with Cavallo, and give your horse the comfort they deserve! Table of Contents Transitioning to Barefoot with Cavallo Why Go Barefoot? Common Challenges Cavallo Hoof Boot Pads Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Transitioning to Barefoot with Cavallo: What You Need to Know Transitioning to barefoot with Cavallo is one of the best decisions you can make for your horse’s health! Moving away from metal shoes allows your horse’s hooves to function as nature intended - flexible, shock-absorbing, and whole. When metal shoes are removed, some horses may experience tenderness or soreness. That’s because circulation and sensation are returning to areas that were previously restricted. During this period, hoof walls may chip, frogs may shed, and your horse might appear uneven on gravel or pavement. This is normal - don’t panic! As your

title: Sizing and Measuring Cavallo Hoof Boots
h1: Sizing and Measuring Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: Sizing and Measuring Cavallo Hoof Boots Sizing and measuring with Cavallo Hoof Boots is simple, but it’s the most crucial step to ensure your horse gets a secure and comfortable fit. Therefore, with just a fresh trim and a few easy measurements, you can choose the right size and sole shape. Whether your horse is barefoot or shod, Cavallo makes it easy to measure correctly and get the boots that support natural movement and total hoof comfort. Table of Contents Why Accurate Measuring Matters Step-by-Step How to Measure Your Horse’s Hooves Upload Your Horse's Measurement Photos Special Cases: Shod Hooves, Fine-Boned Horses & More Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Why Accurate Measuring for Cavallo Hoof Boots Matters Above all else, sizing and measuring properly for Cavallo Hoof Boots is the key to success. A perfect fit keeps the boot snug and secure without rubbing or twisting. Cavallo boots are designed to follow the natural shape of the hoof, so getting these measurements right ensures that your horse gets the comfort and support they deserve. Most Important: Always measure after a fresh trim. The Cavallo size chart already accounts for normal growth between trims, so don’t guess or pad the numbers. title: Navicular Soundness: How Cavallo Boots Can Restore Your Horse's Comfort & Mobility
h1: Navicular Soundness: How Cavallo Boots Can Restore Your Horse's Comfort & Mobility
text: Navicular Soundness: How Cavallo Boots Can Restore Your Horse's Comfort & Mobility Cavallo - The first Ever Riding Boot that Can Double as a Therapy Boot: Since 2006, Cavallo Hoof Boot customers have enjoyed the peace of mind of having a dual-purpose hoof boot—a boot that can handle the toughest of trails yet do double duty as a highly effective therapy boot for Navicular Syndrome or ANY hoof ailment or emergency if ever needed. Having a pair of Cavallos in the barn lets horse owners sleep at night, knowing they’re prepared for anything! Navicular Syndrome: Often Misunderstood Navicular Disease, also known as Navicular Syndrome or Caudal Heel Pain, is a frequently diagnosed yet often misunderstood condition. Its symptoms emerge gradually, manifesting in subtle ways: pinned ears, tail swishing, reluctance to pick up the correct lead, an uneasy stance, or even tripping on uneven ground. Your horse may seem slightly off, but pinpointing the cause can be difficult. Signs such as shifting weight, an unwillingness to stand square, or reluctance to lift a hoof for the farrier should not be overlooked—they are early warnings of a deeper issue. A Natural Solution for Hoof Pain & Injury Conventional treatments often involve stall rest, modified exercise routines, anti-inflammatory medications, or bar shoes. While these methods may provide temporary relief, they do not address the root ca

title: February 2025 Cavallo Newsletter
text: Enjoy Your February 2025 Cavallo News! Guess Who Won the SPOGA Top Innovations AWARD! ???? BREAKING NEWS from Spoga Horse! ???? Cavallo Hoof Boots and Consteed have just made history! We are beyond thrilled to announce that Greg Giles, CEO of Cavallo and Henner Horschen of Continental, have been awarded the prestigious TOP INNOVATIONS 2025 award at Spoga Horse, selected from 31 outstanding nominees! They have won this prestigious award for the co-branded Consteed smart hoof sneaker! Spoga Horse in Germany is the leading innovation trade fair of the international equestrian industry. The Spoga Top Innovations Award celebrates a groundbreaking leap in equine care—and Consteed is the first and only diagnostic hoof boot capable of reporting and predicting lameness. Combining horse well-being, artificial intelligence, and world-class manufacturing, this revolutionary innovation sets a new gold standard in hoof health. With decades of expertise, Cavallo and Consteed, two esteemed industry leaders, have united to redefine what’s possible in equine technology. The future of preventative hoof care and performance monitoring is here! A huge thank you to Spoga Horse for recognizing this innovation, and huge congratulations to all the nominees, including AJR SPORT, Ariat Europe, Horseware Ireland, LeMieux Ltd, and more. title: Storing Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: Storing Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Storing your Cavallo Hoof Boots properly helps extend their life and maintain their performance. Whether you use them daily or just occasionally, proper storage prevents wear, damage, and odour. But with just a few simple habits, you can keep your boots in top condition between rides! In short, this guide covers everything you need to know about cleaning, drying, and storing your Cavallo Hoof Boots the right way. Table of Contents Pre-Storage Considerations Where to Store Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Storing Cavallo Hoof Boots in an Appropriate Environment Special Consideration: What Is Hydrolysis? Cavallo Testimonial s Why Choose Cavallo Hoof Boots Pre-Storage Considerations for Storing Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Before storing your Cavallo Hoof Boots, always clean and dry them thoroughly to prevent damage. Mud, manure, and moisture trapped in the boots can damage the materials and increase the risk of hydrolysis, a chemical breakdown that weakens the sole’s structure over time. Cavallo moulds its hoof boot soles from industrial-grade TPU (thermoplastic urethane), a durable material that can become vulnerable to hydrolysis when stored in humid, enclosed spaces for extended periods. After each use, rinse away dirt and debris with water. Use mild soap if needed. After that, allow the boots to air dry thoroughly in a shaded, well-ventilated area. Never dry
